title: Resolving Payment Failures on Your CloudDash Account
content:
When a payment fails on your CloudDash subscription, the platform enters a dunning process designed to give you time to update payment information before service interruption.

Payment Failure Notification: CloudDash sends an email to all organization Admins immediately when a charge fails. The email includes the failure reason (insufficient funds, expired card, card declined by issuer, or authentication required) and a direct link to update payment methods.

Grace Period: Your account remains fully functional for 7 days after the first failed payment. During this period, a banner appears in the CloudDash console reminding Admins to update billing information. CloudDash retries the charge automatically on days 3, 5, and 7 using the existing payment method.

Updating Payment Method: Go to Settings > Billing > Payment Methods. Add a new credit card or update the existing one. CloudDash accepts Visa, Mastercard, American Express, and Discover. Enterprise customers with invoice-based billing should contact accounts-receivable@clouddash.io if payment terms need adjustment.

3D Secure / SCA Authentication: European customers may encounter Strong Customer Authentication requirements. If payment fails with 'authentication_required', click the link in the failure email to complete 3D Secure verification in your bank's portal, then retry the payment manually from Billing settings.

Account Suspension: If payment is not resolved after 7 days, CloudDash suspends the account. Suspended accounts retain all data for 30 days but lose access to dashboards, alerts, and API. Alert notifications stop firing during suspension. Updating payment during suspension reactivates the account within 15 minutes.

Data Deletion: Accounts suspended for more than 30 days without payment resolution are permanently deleted per our Terms of Service. Enterprise customers with active MSAs have extended retention periods defined in their contract.
